title: José Mariano Jiménez

(August 18, 1781 – June 26, 1811) was a Mexican engineer and rebel [officer](/wiki/Officer_(armed_forces) "Officer (armed forces)") active at the beginning of the [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence").

Martel, Gordon (17 January 2012). [*The Encyclopedia of War, 5 Volume Set*](https://www.google.co.uk/books/edition/The_Encyclopedia_of_War_5_Volume_Set/48hAEAAAQBAJ). John Wiley & Sons. p. 994. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

For other uses, see [José Mariano Jiménez, Chihuahua](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Mariano_Jim%C3%A9nez,_Chihuahua "José Mariano Jiménez, Chihuahua").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing Spanish Wikipedia article at [[:es:José Mariano Jiménez]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|es|José Mariano Jiménez}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Jos%C3%A9_Mariano_Jim%C3%A9nez "Talk:José Mariano Jiménez"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation"). |

**José Mariano Jiménez** (August 18, 1781 – June 26, 1811) was a Mexican engineer and rebel [officer](/wiki/Officer_(armed_forces) "Officer (armed forces)") active at the beginning of the [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/Mariano_Jimenez_statue.jpg/250px-Mariano_Jimenez_statue.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mariano_Jimenez_statue.jpg)

Statue of Jiménez in Chihuahua.

Mariano Jiménez was born in [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD,_San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD "San Luis Potosí, San Luis Potosí"). He studied at the [Colegio de Minería](/w/index.php?title=Colegio_de_Miner%C3%ADa&action=edit&redlink=1 "Colegio de Minería (page does not exist)") in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City") to become a [mining engineer](/wiki/Mine_engineer "Mine engineer"). He graduated in 1804. He soon moved to [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato,_Guanajuato "Guanajuato, Guanajuato"), where he was able to follow the progress of the conspiracy led by [Miguel Hidalgo](/wiki/Miguel_Hidalgo "Miguel Hidalgo") that aimed to achieve the independence of Mexico from [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"). A few days after the war started, Jiménez joined Hidalgo's army and was able to rise quickly in rank until he achieved the rank of [lieutenant colonel](/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel "Lieutenant colonel").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He distinguished himself on the battlefield, such as during the [Battle of Monte de las Cruces](/wiki/Battle_of_Monte_de_las_Cruces "Battle of Monte de las Cruces") where his positioning of the [artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery") line was instrumental for the rebel victory. He was a very active officer and participated in the defense of [Guadalajara](/wiki/Guadalajara,_Jalisco "Guadalajara, Jalisco") and San Luis Potosí.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Mariano Jiménez went to [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") city to ask for the surrender of the city but the viceroy denied it, he went back to [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato") city and participated in her defense because the city was attacked by [Calleja](/wiki/Calleja "Calleja") on November 24, after he went to [Guadalajara](/wiki/Guadalajara "Guadalajara") and [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD "San Luis Potosí").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

A betrayal by one of his subordinates led to his capture at the [Wells of Baján](/wiki/Wells_of_Baj%C3%A1n "Wells of Baján") (*Norias de Baján*) in [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila") in 1811. He was tried for insubordination and executed by [firing squad](/wiki/Firing_squad "Firing squad") on June 26, 1811,[[1]](#cite_note-1) together with other members of the insurgency such as [Ignacio Allende](/wiki/Ignacio_Allende "Ignacio Allende") and [Juan Aldama](/wiki/Juan_Aldama "Juan Aldama") in [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua,_Chihuahua "Chihuahua, Chihuahua").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Jiménez's body was decapitated and his head taken to the [Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Alhóndiga de Granaditas"), where it was shown to the public inside a cage hung from one corner of the building.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1925, his remains were moved to the mausoleum in the [Independence Column](/wiki/El_%C3%81ngel "El Ángel") in Mexico City.
